introduction:
A Death Certificate in Karnataka is issued by the local Municipal Corporation, Town Panchayat, or Gram Panchayat through the Nadakacheri (Atalji Janasnehi Kendra) portal. It is the official proof of death and is governed by the Registration of Births and Deaths Act, 1969.

This certificate is essential for insurance claims, property transfer, bank account closure, pension settlement, and succession proceedings. Karnataka uses the eJanma system for digital registration in rural areas and the Nadakacheri portal for certificate issuance.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Death must be registered within 21 days (free of charge)
  • After 21 days: late fee + Registrar's permission required
  • After 1 year: Executive Magistrate's order required
  • First copy is FREE; duplicates cost ₹5–₹25
  • Track at nadakacheri.karnataka.gov.in or crsorgi.gov.in
  • Helpline: 080-22230282 (Directorate of Municipal Administration)
documentsRequired:
Documents Required for Death Certificate

Note: Carry originals for verification and submit self-attested photocopies.
Proof of Death
  • Medical Certificate of Cause of Death (MCCD)
  • Hospital death summary
  • Cremation / Burial receipt
  • Police report (for unnatural deaths)
ID Proof of Deceased
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Voter ID
  • PAN Card
  • Passport
  • Driving Licence
  • Ration Card
Informant Documents
  • Aadhaar of informant
  • Relationship proof
  • Address proof
For Delayed Registration
  • Affidavit on stamp paper
  • Magistrate order (after 1 year)
  • Non-availability certificate
  • Supporting death proof
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via Nadakacheri / CRS Portal

Karnataka provides online death registration through Nadakacheri and the national CRS portal.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit the Portal
    Go to nadakacheri.karnataka.gov.in or crsorgi.gov.in
  2. Register / Login
    Create account, verify via OTP.
  3. Select "Death Registration"
    Navigate to Online Services → Death Certificate.
  4. Fill the Form
    Enter deceased details, cause of death, informant info, jurisdiction.
  5. Upload Documents
    Upload MCCD, ID proof. Max 200 KB (JPG/PDF)
  6. Submit & Get Reference
    Note your Acknowledgement Number.
  7. Verification
    Village Accountant / Municipal officer verifies within 7–15 days.
Tip: Verify the certificate using acknowledgement number on the Nadakacheri website.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Register Offline at Municipal / Gram Panchayat Office

Visit the local Municipal Office, Town Panchayat, or Gram Panchayat.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit Registration Office
    Hours: Mon–Sat, 10 AM – 5 PM
  2. Collect Form
    Get Death Registration Form (Form No. 2) — free.
  3. Fill & Attach Documents
    Fill in black ink, attach photocopies, carry originals.
  4. Submit & Get Receipt
    Receive Acknowledgement with registration number.
  5. Collect Certificate
    Collect within 7–15 working days.
Processing Time: 7–15 working days for timely registration.
afterApplying:
After Applying — What Next?

Track Status

Visit Nadakacheri to track.

Download Certificate

Download digitally signed certificate from the portal.

Legal Use

Use for insurance, property, bank, pension, and succession matters.

Grievance

Call 080-22230282 or visit the local municipal office.
